translated: ProSeka AfterTalk: Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow.
Good evening, everyone.
I'm Hinata Sato, the voice of Mizuki Akiyama from Nightcord at 25:00.
This ProSeka AfterTalk is a stream where we look back on the event while reading the letters you've sent in.
This time, we're covering the event "Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow."
I hope you all enjoyed the story.
The event just ended yesterday, so how was it for everyone?
Tell me what you thought.
I really hope you all enjoyed it.
It was a story where Mizuki really had to face their future,
and think about what they want to do later in life.
I think it might have really resonated with those of you who are currently deciding on your own career paths or higher education.
Also, AfterTalk got a bit of a renewal starting in April.
We've been collecting letters for the show via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
Thank you so much to everyone who sent one in.
I'll be reading some of your letters during the show, so please look forward to that.
If you want to share your thoughts on the event or this stream, please post using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm keeping an eye on the YouTube chat, and I'll check the hashtag later too,
so I'll try to pick out any comments that catch my eye.
There are so many international comments! I can barely find the Japanese ones. People are saying "The ribbon is cute" or "I have the same hairstyle as Mizuki."
The stream might be lagging a bit,
but I see people mentioning my hair and outfit.
I tried to match the card illustration for today's look—well, I always do that, actually.
I definitely had the illustration in mind.
Good evening! Thank you all for joining.
Oh, someone said, "The story and the new song were so good I bawled my eyes out."
I'll talk about the song later.
I was at home shouting, "What is this?!" when I saw it.
Like, "What even is this?!"
I really felt the incredible effort the team put into it.
But yes, I'll save that talk for later.
Now, let's dive right into the story for "Reaching Out to an Unraveling Tomorrow."
We've received letters about this story as well,
so let's look back on the event together while I read them.
[CORNER: Fan Letter Corner] First, we have a letter from Kotori no Mama-san.
"Hello, Hinatsu-san! Kon-Niigo!"
Kon-Niigo!
"My child and I enjoy playing ProSeka together."
"Mizuki-chan's banner was incredibly cute, funny, and moving."
"Through their past experiences and interactions with those around them,"
"I'm glad we got to see so many of Mizuki-chan's different feelings."
"I think staying true to yourself and not giving up on what you love can be hard when you hit walls or feel lost."
"But I believe that if you hold onto those feelings, a time will come when they are rewarded."
"I believe there are definitely people out there, like the members of Niigo,"
"who will see and accept you for who you truly are."
"I really love the stories in ProSeka."
"Hinatsu-san, thank you so much for always giving them a voice."
"I'll continue to support both Mizuki-chan and Hinatsu-san with all I've got!"
Thank you so much, Kotori no Mama-san.
In this story, it starts with Mizuki's older sister coming home from abroad to open a pop-up shop.
Mizuki wanting the other Niigo members to meet their sister felt like a huge step forward for them.
Kanade and Mafuyu had met her before,
but Ena got to meet her for the first time this time. The story really progressed in various ways.
Mizuki loves making videos, but they also love
remaking clothes, visiting boutiques,
and looking at makeup. They have so many things they love.
I think it's amazing that they're already facing the question of what they want to pursue as a career at this stage.
When I was looking at colleges, I just thought, "If I'm paying for this, I need to study something that gives me a job and tangible skills," so I just went to tons of open campuses.
My approach to finding what I liked was completely different from Mizuki's.
But I think this was a vital time for Mizuki to re-examine what they love at their own pace.
It wasn't just about their sister's pop-up shop;
realizing that their love for clothes might be something they want to turn into a career is a huge deal. I'm just glad they could end up smiling.
